title: Bipartite Testing using BFS
description: Algorithm to check if a graph is bipartite using Breadth-First Search and 2-coloring
code:
FUNCTION isBipartite(graph G, vertices n)
 // Initialize color array: -1 = uncolored, 0 = first color, 1 = second color
 DECLARE color[n]
 FOR i = 0 to n-1 DO
 color[i] = -1
 END FOR

 // Graph may be disconnected, check all components
 FOR i = 0 to n-1 DO
 IF color[i] == -1 THEN
 // Start BFS from this vertex
 color[i] = 0
 CREATE queue q
 q.enqueue(i)

 WHILE q is not empty DO
 u = q.dequeue()
 FOR each neighbor v of u DO
 IF color[v] == -1 THEN
 // Assign opposite color
 color[v] = 1 - color[u]
 q.enqueue(v)
 ELSE IF color[v] == color[u] THEN
 // Same color adjacent - NOT bipartite
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN TRUE
END FUNCTION
explanation: The algorithm uses BFS to traverse the graph and assigns colors to vertices. If we find two adjacent vertices with the same color, the graph contains an odd cycle and is not bipartite. The time complexity is O(V+E) where V is vertices and E is edges.

title: Count Edges in K(m,n)
description: Simple algorithm to calculate number of edges in complete bipartite graph
explanation: In a complete bipartite graph K(m,n), each of the m vertices in the first partition is connected to all n vertices in the second partition. Since there are m vertices each with n edges, total edges = m × n.

title: Find Maximum Matching in Bipartite Graph
description: Hungarian algorithm or augmenting path method for maximum bipartite matching
code:
// Simple augmenting path algorithm for maximum matching
FUNCTION maxBipartiteMatching(graph G, partitions U, V)
 DECLARE matchU[|U|] initialized to -1 // matched vertex in V for each U
 DECLARE matchV[|V|] initialized to -1 // matched vertex in U for each V
 result = 0

 FUNCTION bfsFindAugmentingPath()
 DECLARE visited[|U|] initialized to FALSE
 DECLARE queue q
 
 FOR each u in U DO
 IF matchU[u] == -1 THEN
 visited[u] = TRUE
 q.enqueue(u)
 END IF
 END FOR

 WHILE q not empty DO
 u = q.dequeue()
 FOR each v neighbor of u DO
 // If v is unmatched or we can find augmenting path
 IF matchV[v] == -1 OR 
 (NOT visited[matchV[v]] AND bfsFindPathFrom(matchV[v])) THEN
 matchU[u] = v
 matchV[v] = u
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 RETURN FALSE
 END FUNCTION

 // Try to find augmenting paths until none exist
 WHILE bfsFindAugmentingPath() DO
 result = result + 1
 END WHILE

 RETURN result
END FUNCTION
explanation: This algorithm finds maximum matching in a bipartite graph using augmenting paths. A matching is maximum if and only if there is no augmenting path. The algorithm repeatedly finds augmenting paths until none exist. Time complexity is O(VE) for simple implementation.
